behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (inventory availability filter + ranker), not just one function. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a unit test of `excludeOutOfStock()` passes, but integrated pipeline still returns an out-of-stock SKU because the ranker merges a prefiltered candidate list from another module. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no AI/ML output-quality dependency, no multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text only describes local filtering/ranking logic with deterministic business rules.
behaviors.test_description: Wire catalog loader, availability filter, and ranker in-process with fixture data where an out-of-stock item has the top relevance score; assert final recommendations never include it.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when user-settings module hands blocked categories to filtering module. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: category-filter unit test passes, but integration fails because settings service maps category IDs differently and blocked categories leak through. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no OS/network/subprocess needed; no model-output dependence; no multi-step agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected higher levels because behavior text is deterministic preference exclusion only.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender pipeline with in-process user-settings repository + filter + ranker; provide blocked categories in user profile and assert none appear in output.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug can occur in feature extraction -> scoring handoff, not only inside one scoring function. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: scorer unit test passes with precomputed features, but integrated run fails because browsing-event featurizer drops recency windows, so matching items are not boosted. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real infrastructure boundary required; no LLM/AI nondeterminism; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic because behavior does not involve a real AI/ML service call; it is deterministic ranking logic.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process browsing-history store, feature builder, and ranker with fixtures where one product has strong recent browsing signal and another has none; assert boosted product ranks above baseline.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: stability depends on sorter + paginator + repository ordering contract. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: paginator unit test passes on already-sorted input, but integrated call returns different page-2 items between runs because tie-break ordering from upstream module is unstable. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory OS/network/subprocess boundary; no AI/ML output dependence; no multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System since behavior text does not require real HTTP/process/OS effects, only local ordering consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Run full in-process query->sort->paginate flow with tied scores and deterministic fixture data; request page 2 twice without mutations and assert identical item IDs/order each time.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is often in interaction between core ranker and diversity/serendipity constraint layer. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: unit test for diversity rule passes in isolation, but integrated ranking post-processing truncates candidate list and removes all unseen-category items. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement; no LLM-dependent judgment; no chained agentic steps. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text specifies deterministic recommendation policy, not model-output quality from a real AI API.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate candidate generation, ranking, and diversity enforcement in-process with fixtures where top relevance items are all familiar categories; assert final list contains at least one unseen-category item.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at cold-start detection + fallback strategy handoff. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: fallback selector unit test passes, but integrated pipeline returns empty results because popularity source is not invoked when history arrays are null vs empty. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI-model output dependency; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic fallback routing.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process recommender with empty user history and populated popularity index; assert non-empty recommendations come from popularity ordering path.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug arises when recommendation response metadata is handed to tracking/event persistence modules. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: unit test for event serializer passes, but integrated flow stores wrong `position` because UI payload index is 0-based while analytics schema expects 1-based. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory external OS/network/subprocess behavior in spec; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require validating real external analytics transport.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise in-process recommendation-serving + click-event builder + event store; simulate click on known rank position and assert stored product ID, rank position, and timestamp fields match expectation.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: latency behavior depends on real process/runtime/OS scheduling and realistic memory/CPU effects; in-process fakes can hide true timing regressions. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: integration test with mocked repository and tiny fixture passes under 200ms, while real process on 100k catalog exceeds 200ms due to serialization and allocation overhead. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly sets end-to-end latency SLO at production-like scale, which needs real runtime measurement. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML or agent pipeline is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run a system performance test in a real process with a 100k-product dataset and production-like configuration; issue recommendation requests for representative users and assert p95 latency remains below 200ms.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs across purchase-event ingestion, user-state update, and ranker weighting. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: unit test of penalty scoring passes, but integrated flow still ranks purchased item high because purchase signal is written to orders table but recommender reads stale profile state. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; no LLM/model-output quality dependency; no multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System due lack of explicit external infra dependency in the behavior itself.
behaviors.test_description: In-process integration: generate recommendations, record purchase event for a recommended SKU, regenerate recommendations using updated user state, and assert purchased SKU rank decreases relative to prior response.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: determinism depends on full pipeline state handoff (candidate retrieval, tie-break sorting, and ranking seed handling), not one isolated function. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: unit test for deterministic comparator passes, but integrated requests differ because upstream module iterates an unordered collection before sorting tied scores. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required OS/network/subprocess boundary; no AI/ML nondeterministic model call; no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System/Agentic/Workflow because behavior text defines deterministic local recommendation behavior without external model calls.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full in-process recommendation pipeline twice with identical user snapshot and unchanged catalog; assert exact same ordered product ID list across both calls.
